name: Quaternion
summary: Represents the four coefficient values in a quaternion. The first value a is the real part, while the rest multiplies i, j and k, that are imaginary.quaternion = a + bi + cj + dk

constructors.name: Quaternion
constructors.signature: public Quaternion(double a, double b, double c, double d)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new quaternion with the provided coefficients.

params.name: a
params.doc: A number. This is the real part.

params.name: b
params.doc: Another number. This is the first coefficient of the imaginary part.

params.name: c
params.doc: Another number. This is the second coefficient of the imaginary part.

params.name: d
params.doc: Another number. This is the third coefficient of the imaginary part.

methods.name: CrossProduct
methods.signature: public static Quaternion CrossProduct(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)
methods.summary: Computes the vector cross product of p and q = (0,x,y,z), where (x,y,z) = CrossProduct(p.Vector,q.Vector).This is not the same as the quaternion product p*q.

methods.name: Distance
methods.signature: public static double Distance(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)
methods.summary: Returns the distance or norm of the difference between two quaternions.

methods.name: DistanceTo
methods.signature: public double DistanceTo(Quaternion q)
methods.summary: Computes the distance or norm of the difference between this and another quaternion.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(Quaternion other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.signature: public override bool Equals(Object obj)
methods.summary: Determines whether an object is a quaternion and has the same value of this quaternion.

returns.doc: true if obj is a quaternion and has exactly equal coefficients; otherwise false.

methods.name: Equals(Quaternion)
methods.signature: public bool Equals(Quaternion other)
methods.summary: Determines whether this quaternion has the same value of another quaternion.

returns.doc: true if the quaternions have exactly equal coefficients; otherwise false.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.signature: public override int GetHashCode()
methods.summary: Gets a non-unique but repeatable hashing code for this quaternion.

methods.name: GetRotation(Plane)
methods.signature: public bool GetRotation(out Plane plane)
methods.summary: Returns the frame created by applying the quaternion's rotation to the canonical world frame (1,0,0),(0,1,0),(0,0,1).

params.name: plane
params.doc: A plane. This out value will be assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GetRotation(Transform)
methods.signature: public bool GetRotation(out Transform xform)
methods.summary: Returns a transformation matrix that performs the rotation defined by the quaternion. The transformation returned by this method has the property that xform * V = q.Rotate(V). If the quaternion is not unitized, the rotation of its unitized form is returned.
methods.remarks: Do not confuse the result of this method the transformation matrix returned by MatrixForm().

methods.name: GetRotation(Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public bool GetRotation(out double angle, out Vector3d axis)
methods.summary: Returns the rotation defined by the quaternion.
methods.remarks: If the quaternion is not unitized, the rotation of its unitized form is returned.

params.name: axis
params.doc: unit axis of rotation of 0 if (b,c,d) is the zero vector.

returns.doc: True if the operation succeeded; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Invert
methods.signature: public bool Invert()
methods.summary: Modifies this quaternion to become (a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2),where L2 = length squared = (a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d).This is the multiplicative inverse, i.e., (a,b,c,d)*(a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2) = (1,0,0,0).

returns.doc: true if successful. false if the quaternion is zero and cannot be inverted.

methods.name: MatrixForm
methods.signature: public Transform MatrixForm()
methods.summary: Returns 4x4 real valued matrix form of the quaternion a b c d -b a -d c -c d a -b -d -c b a which has the same arithmetic properties as the quaternion.
methods.remarks: Do not confuse this with the rotation defined by the quaternion. This function will only be interesting to math nerds and is not useful in rendering or animation applications.

methods.name: Product
methods.signature: public static Quaternion Product(Quaternion p, Quaternion q)
methods.summary: The quaternion product of p and q. This is the same value as p*q.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.signature: public Vector3d Rotate(Vector3d v)
methods.summary: Rotates a 3d vector. This operation is also called conjugation, because the result is the same as (q.Conjugate()*(0,x,y,x)*q/q.LengthSquared).Vector.
methods.remarks: If you need to rotate more than a dozen or so vectors, it will be more efficient to calculate the rotation matrix once and use it repeatedly.

returns.doc: R*v, where R is the rotation defined by the unit quaternion. This is mathematically the same as the values (Inverse(q)*(0,x,y,z)*q).Vector and (q.Conjugate()*(0,x,y,x)*q/q.LengthSquared).Vector.

methods.name: Rotation(Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion Rotation(double angle, Vector3d axisOfRotation)
methods.summary: Returns the unit quaternion cos(angle/2), sin(angle/2)*x, sin(angle/2)*y, sin(angle/2)*z where (x,y,z) is the unit vector parallel to axis. This is the unit quaternion that represents the rotation of angle about axis.

methods.name: Rotation(Plane, Plane)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion Rotation(Plane plane0, Plane plane1)
methods.summary: Returns the unit quaternion that represents the rotation that maps plane0.xaxis to plane1.xaxis, plane0.yaxis to plane1.yaxis, and plane0.zaxis to plane1.zaxis.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: public void Set(double a, double b, double c, double d)
methods.summary: Sets all coefficients of the quaternion.

methods.name: SetRotation(Double, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public void SetRotation(double angle, Vector3d axisOfRotation)
methods.summary: Sets the quaternion to cos(angle/2), sin(angle/2)*x, sin(angle/2)*y, sin(angle/2)*z where (x,y,z) is the unit vector parallel to axis. This is the unit quaternion that represents the rotation of angle about axis.

params.name: axisOfRotation
params.doc: The direction of the axis of rotation.

methods.name: SetRotation(Plane, Plane)
methods.signature: public void SetRotation(Plane plane0, Plane plane1)
methods.summary: Sets the quaternion to the unit quaternion which rotates plane0.xaxis to plane1.xaxis, plane0.yaxis to plane1.yaxis, and plane0.zaxis to plane1.zaxis.

params.name: plane0
params.doc: The "from" rotation plane. Origin point is ignored.

params.name: plane1
params.doc: The "to" rotation plane. Origin point is ignored.

methods.name: ToString
methods.signature: public override string ToString()
methods.summary: Returns a string representation of this Quaternion.

methods.name: Unitize
methods.signature: public bool Unitize()
methods.summary: Scales the quaternion's coordinates so that a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d = 1.

returns.doc: true if successful. false if the quaternion is zero and cannot be unitized.

methods.name: Addition
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator +(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)
methods.summary: Adds two quaternions. This sums each quaternion coefficient with its correspondent and returns a new result quaternion.

methods.name: Division
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator /(Quaternion q, double y)
methods.summary: Divides all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two quaternions have the same value.

returns.doc: true if the quaternions have exactly equal coefficients; otherwise false.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two quaternions have different values.

returns.doc: true if the quaternions differ in any coefficient; otherwise false.

methods.name: Multiply(Quaternion, Quaternion)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator *(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)
methods.summary: Multiplies a quaternion with another one. Quaternion multiplication (Hamilton product) is not commutative.

methods.name: Multiply(Quaternion, Double)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator *(Quaternion q, double x)
methods.summary: Multiplies (scales) all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

methods.name: Multiply(Quaternion, Int32)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator *(Quaternion q, int x)
methods.summary: Multiplies (scales) all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

methods.name: Multiply(Quaternion, Single)
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator *(Quaternion q, float x)
methods.summary: Multiplies (scales) all quaternion coefficients by a factor and returns a new quaternion with the result.

methods.name: Subtraction
methods.signature: public static Quaternion operator -(Quaternion a, Quaternion b)
methods.summary: Subtracts a quaternion from another one. This computes the difference of each quaternion coefficient with its correspondent and returns a new result quaternion.

properties.name: A
properties.summary: Gets or sets the real part of the quaternion.

properties.name: B
properties.summary: Gets or sets the first imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.

properties.name: C
properties.summary: Gets or sets the second imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.

properties.name: Conjugate
properties.summary: Gets a new quaternion that is the conjugate of this quaternion. This is (a,-b,-c,-d)

properties.name: D
properties.summary: Gets or sets the third imaginary coefficient of the quaternion.

properties.name: Inverse
properties.summary: Computes a new inverted quaternion, (a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2),where L2 = length squared = (a*a + b*b + c*c + d*d). This is the multiplicative inverse, i.e., (a,b,c,d)*(a/L2, -b/L2, -c/L2, -d/L2) = (1,0,0,0). If this is the zero quaternion, then the zero quaternion is returned.

properties.name: IsScalar
properties.summary: true if b, c, and d are all zero.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Determines if the four coefficients are valid numbers within RhinoCommon. See IsValidDouble(Double).

properties.name: IsVector
properties.summary: true if a = 0 and at least one of b, c, or d is not zero.

properties.name: IsZero
properties.summary: true if a, b, c, and d are all zero.

properties.name: Length
properties.summary: Returns the length or norm of the quaternion.

properties.name: LengthSquared
properties.summary: Gets the result of (a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + d^2).

properties.name: Scalar
properties.summary: The real (scalar) part of the quaternion This is A.

properties.name: Vector
properties.summary: The imaginary part of the quaternion (B,C,D)

properties.name: Zero
properties.summary: Returns the default quaternion, where all coefficients are 0.
